name: Támara de Campos y Santoyo – circular desde Astudillo
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Astudillo traverse the Castilian plains, characterized by rolling agricultural fields and river valleys. The region offers varied terrain suitable for cycling, with routes often connecting historical towns and landmarks. Cyclists can expect a landscape of open vistas and gentle elevation changes, typical of the Palencia province. Best touring cycling routes around Astudillo The most popular touring cycling route is Canal de Castilla – circular desde Fròmista por Osorno, a 25.8 miles (41.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, offering flat sections and views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop, a moderate 19.6 miles (31.5 km) path. This route combines historical castle views with sections along the Canal de Castilla. Local touring cyclists also love the Támara de Campos y Santoyo – circular desde Astudillo, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail leading through rural villages and agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Astudillo is defined by open agricultural fields, river valleys, and historical villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Astudillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Astudillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Astudillo?
A: Touring cycling around Astudillo primarily features the Castilian plains, characterized by rolling agricultural fields and river valleys. You'll find a mix of open vistas and gentle elevation changes, typical of the Palencia province. Some routes may include gravel sections, offering a varied experience for cyclists.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Astudillo?
A: Yes, Astudillo offers routes suitable for various ability levels. While many routes are classified as moderate, there are options with gentler elevation changes that can be enjoyed by beginners. For example, routes along the Canal de Castilla often provide flatter sections.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes near Astudillo?
A: The region is rich in history. You can encounter several historical landmarks, including sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; and its impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4541372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista&lt;/a&gt;, known for its Romanesque church, is also accessible on some routes. Additionally, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/849948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás Hermitage at Puente Fitero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Astudillo?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170915540/tamara-de-campos-y-santoyo-circular-desde-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Támara de Campos y Santoyo – circular desde Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 33.3 km trail leading through rural villages and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Astudillo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Astudillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the scenic beauty of the Castilian plains, the historical points of interest, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Astudillo area?
A: There are several touring cycling routes available around Astudillo, offering a good selection for exploration. Komoot lists a number of options, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails to suit various preferences.

Q: Can I find routes that combine historical sites with natural landscapes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the Astudillo area offer a blend of historical and natural sights. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170901608/monzon-de-campos-castle-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; combines views of a historic castle with sections along the picturesque Canal de Castilla.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes in Astudillo?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, there are longer and more difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks – Frómista loop from El Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 79.1 km trail, offering a substantial ride for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in Astudillo?
A: Route durations vary depending on length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170888784/canal-de-castilla-circular-desde-fromista-por-osorno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla – circular desde Fròmista por Osorno&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 10 minutes to complete, while shorter routes can be done in under 2 hours.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through rural villages?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to take you through the charming rural villages surrounding Astudillo, allowing you to experience the local culture and architecture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175519652/san-cebrian-de-campos-and-man" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cebrián de Campos and Man&lt;/a&gt; route is one such example, offering a glimpse into the countryside life.

Q: What are the best natural features to look out for on Astudillo's cycling routes?
A: The primary natural feature is the expansive Castilian landscape itself, with its rolling agricultural fields and open vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, while man-made, is a significant feature that blends seamlessly with the natural environment, offering peaceful waterside cycling and a unique ecosystem.
tips: Beautiful little castle surrounded by underground mountain dwellings

The church of San Hipólito el Real in Támara de Campo is an impressive temple built in the 14th century. Its imposing structure stands out in the landscape of Tierra de Campos. With Gothic and Renaissance architectural elements, its construction took place for more than a hundred years, ending in the 16th century.
